MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANTS VIRGINIA DUARTE AND LUIS DUARTE'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT [ATTY AFFIDAVIT OF FAULT] AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE ANSWER.
APPEARANCE: James M. Millar, Esq. of Law Offices of James M. Millar 981-8100 for deft/cross-complainant & opposing party Theodore Usher; John E. Jones, Esq. of Law Offices of John E. Jones 673-0733 for cross-defts & moving parties (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: Argued and the Court granted the motion. Moving party to submit proposed order & comply with CRC 391. Proposed order to include blank lines for attorney fees & costs, and for sanctions; amounts to be determined by the Court. Proposed pleading to be filed 10 days after notice of entry of order. (302/REQ/JT) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2006 Continued To Mar-30-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up As To Cross Complaint In Scd.
Off Calendar Jan-12-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Cross Complaint/Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Beldon Yee.
James Milar, Esq. for Cross Complainant: Theodore Usher, present, was sworn and examined.
After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Cross-Complainant and against the Defaulted Cross-Defendants: Virginia Duarte and Luis Duarte as follows:
Damages $25,457.18
Attorney's Fees $10,372.40
Costs $289.20, for a total of $39,771.06
Counsel to prepare Judgment.

2:00 P.M.
Judgment signed by Comm. Kaplan.
